metadata.description: EtherFi is a liquid staking protocol that issues eETH (and its wrapped variant weETH) against ETH deposits. Staked ETH is restaked into EigenLayer strategies and Lido stETH positions, earning both consensus-layer and restaking rewards. The protocol is fully upgradeable: most core contracts sit behind EIP-1967 proxies controlled by a 3-day timelock, with a secondary 8-hour timelock governing operational parameters. A 3/5 multisig and a 4/7 multisig share proposer/executor roles on these timelocks, and several EOAs hold pauser or node-operator privileges. External dependencies include EigenLayer (delegation, pod management, strategy deposits), Lido (stETH minting and withdrawals), Curve (liquidity pools), Coinbase cbETH, and LayerZero (cross-chain messaging). Total capital at risk across admins is $751.62M.

admins.name: EtherFi Governance 4/7 Multisig
admins.description: A 4-of-7 multisig that holds proposer and executor roles on the main 3-day EtherFiTimelock. Can schedule, execute, and cancel timelock transactions. Does not control any contracts directly — all actions are gated by the timelock delay.

mitigations.description: Delay before execution
Delay before execution

admins.name: EtherFi Main Timelock (3d)
admins.description: Primary governance timelock with a 3-day delay. Controls upgrades to the majority of protocol contracts (LiquidityPool, EtherFiNodesManager, StakingManager, TNFT, WithdrawRequestNFT, Liquifier, MembershipManager, and others), manages EtherFiRestaker EigenLayer operations (delegation, strategy deposits, withdrawals), and governs protocol parameters across AuctionManager, EtherFiOracle, ProtocolRevenueManager, and MembershipNFT. Holds proposer/executor roles shared with the 4/7 multisig. Controls $751.62M in direct capital exposure across 155 permissioned functions.

admins.name: Zero Address (Renounced)
admins.description: Represents renounced permissions. Holds minting rights on DummyTokenUpgradeable, acceptOwnership on RoleRegistry, and rescueERC20 on external Coinbase cbETH and Curve WrapTokenV3ETH. These permissions are effectively burned.

admins.name: EtherFi Operations 3/5 Multisig
admins.description: A 3-of-5 multisig with direct control over operational functions: pausing/unpausing LiquidityPool, EtherFiAdmin, EtherFiRateLimiter, and EtherFiRedemptionManager; managing DummyTokenUpgradeable roles; triggering batch validator creation; and configuring rate limiter consumers. Can also recover stuck ETH/ERC20/ERC721 from eETH and weETH token contracts. Controls $0 in direct capital across 35 functions.

mitigations.description: Validator size bounded between 32 ETH and 2048 ETH

admins.name: EtherFiAdmin Contract
admins.description: An upgradeable admin contract that aggregates multiple protocol operations behind a single caller. Manages validator lifecycle (approval, confirmation, funding), LiquidityPool parameters (validator size, fee recipient, restaking config), pause/unpause, and protocol fee payments. Owned by the main 3-day timelock. Controls $180.39M in direct capital.

admins.name: RoleRegistry Contract
admins.description: An upgradeable role-based access control contract that gates operations on WithdrawRequestNFT (finalize/invalidate/validate requests, pause, handle remainders) and Wrapped eETH Token (recover assets, upgrade proxy). The RoleRegistry itself is owned by the main 3-day timelock. Controls $751.62M in reachable capital through withdrawal and weETH management.

dependencies.name: Ethereum Beacon Chain DepositContract
dependencies.description: Ethereum's native Beacon Chain deposit contract. EtherFi deposits 32 ETH per validator through StakingManager to activate new beacon validators. An immutable system contract with no admin risk.

dependencies.name: EigenLayer EigenPodManager
dependencies.description: Manages EigenPod creation and beacon chain proof verification for EtherFi's restaked validators. Called by EtherFiNodesManager and EtherFiRestaker. Upgradeable by EigenLayer governance.

dependencies.name: LayerZero Unknown Contract
dependencies.description: A LayerZero infrastructure contract that interacts with EtherFi's cross-chain messaging components. Writes to protocol state via the OFT adapter layer.

dependencies.name: EigenLayer DelegationManager
dependencies.description: Handles operator delegation and withdrawal queuing for EtherFi's restaked positions. EtherFiRestaker delegates to EigenLayer operators and queues/completes strategy withdrawals through this contract. Upgradeable by EigenLayer governance.

dependencies.name: EigenLayer StrategyManager
dependencies.description: Manages EtherFi's restaking strategy deposits into EigenLayer. EtherFiRestaker deposits stETH into EigenLayer strategies via this contract. The restaker holds $571.20M in total positions across EigenLayer and Lido combined.

dependencies.name: Lido WithdrawalQueueERC721
dependencies.description: Lido's withdrawal queue. EtherFiRestaker requests stETH-to-ETH withdrawals through this contract when rebalancing or exiting Lido positions.

dependencies.name: EigenLayer RewardsCoordinator
dependencies.description: Distributes restaking rewards to EtherFi's restaker contract. EtherFiRestaker sets its rewards claimer through the DelegationManager which interacts with this coordinator.

dependencies.name: Lido stETH Token
dependencies.description: Lido's liquid staked ETH token. The Liquifier contract interacts with stETH for liquidity operations, and EtherFiRestaker holds stETH positions deposited into EigenLayer strategies.

dependencies.name: Coinbase cbETH Token
dependencies.description: Coinbase's wrapped staked ETH token. The Liquifier can accept and process cbETH deposits as an alternative liquid staking token input.

dependencies.name: Curve WrapTokenV3ETH
dependencies.description: Curve wrapper contract used by the Liquifier for token wrapping operations in liquidity management workflows.

dependencies.name: Curve Vyper_contract
dependencies.description: A Curve liquidity pool contract used for token swaps in the protocol's liquidity management operations.

dependencies.name: LayerZero EndpointV2
dependencies.description: LayerZero's cross-chain messaging endpoint. Used by EtherfiL1SyncPoolETH and EtherfiOFTAdapterUpgradeable for cross-chain eETH/weETH bridging and L1-L2 sync operations. Immutable contract with configurable message libraries.

funds.name: LiquidityPool (ETH)
funds.description: The main liquidity pool holding user-deposited ETH awaiting validator deployment or withdrawal processing. Holds $180.39M primarily in ETH, representing the protocol's liquid reserves available for staking and redemptions.

funds.name: Treasury (ETH)
funds.description: Protocol treasury holding accumulated fees and reserves. Contains $23.34K in ETH, controlled by the main 3-day timelock.

funds.name: eETH Token
funds.description: The ether.fi ETH rebasing token contract. eETH represents staked ETH positions with a current market capitalization of $6.06B.

funds.name: Wrapped eETH (weETH)
funds.description: The non-rebasing wrapped eETH token. Holds eETH on behalf of users who wrapped, used for DeFi integrations and cross-chain bridging. Wrapped value is intentionally not added to TVS to avoid double-counting against the eETH market cap.

funds.name: EtherFiRestaker (EigenLayer Positions)
funds.description: Holds EtherFi's restaked positions in EigenLayer and Lido. $571.20M in DeFi positions split between EigenLayer deposits/yield (stETH restaked) and Lido staked ETH. This is the largest single concentration of protocol capital.

governance.votingProcess: Snapshot vote (4-day window, 1M ETHFI quorum). Foundation multisig executes the proposals arbitrarily
